weapons:Daggers:missed.title: Daggers
weapons:Daggers:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Dagger.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are an [[Armament Type]] in {{ER}}.
They generally have very short reach and low damage against both health and poise, but compensates for this with their fast attack speed and sometimes higher-than-average damage on [[Critical Attack]]s, making several of them (particularly the [[Dagger]] and especially the [[Miséricorde]]) powerful tools for [[Critical Attack|critical attacks]]. 

Their fast attack-speed and high number of hits-per-second also makes them good at building up [[Status Effects]] like [[Poison]] and [[Blood Loss]]. 

Due to their general low [[weight]], they also make excellent catalysts for certain [[Ashes of War]] and [[Skills]], providing a stat-efficient means of casting status buffs like [[Skill: Golden Vow|Golden Vow]] or [[Skill: Shared Order|Shared Order]] with no need for high investment in certain attributes, while specific daggers such as [[Reduvia]] and the [[Black Knife]] have exceptionally strong ranged [[Skill]]s that only they posses ([[Unique Skill: Reduvia Blood Blade|Reduvia Blood Blade]] and [[Unique Skill: Blade of Death|Blade of Death]] respectively). 

==Moveset==
The moveset for Daggers consists mostly of rapid slashes, with the exception of one- and two-handed rolling and heavy running attacks, two-handed heavy attacks, and paired backstep attacks, which are all thrusts. 

Some daggers have unique heavy attacks: [[Glintstone Kris]], [[Main-gauche]], [[Miséricorde]], and [[Scorpion's Stinger]] have thrusts, and [[Black Knife]], [[Blade of Calling]], [[Erdsteel Dagger]], and [[Fire Knight's Shortsword]] have a quick, double-hit slash. 

The dual-wield moveset is notably quite quick and both the backstep attack and the jumping attack are excellent due to their reach and speed respectively. The first attack in the dual-wield attack chain is surprisingly slow due to being composed of 3 separate hits, making it somewhat difficult to use since Daggers won

weapons:Straight Swords:missed.title: Straight Swords
weapons:Straight Swords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:{{#setmainimage:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Longsword.png}}|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}. They boast decently quick attack speeds, with reasonable range and area coverage for said speed. Their movesets are consistently very versatile and easy to use, particularly when one-handed and dual-wielded. Two-handing may be slightly less reliable compared to the other ways of using them, but its increased poise-damage still gives it uses in certain situations.

Overall straight swords pair exceptionally well with shields and most other weapon classes in general, as their regular attack's wide and fast horizontal swings help cover for potential shortcomings that other weapon movesets might face.

They also have exclusive access to the [[Skill: Square Off]], a strong and versatile stance skill that easily breaks open blocks with its light attack version and that quickly breaks enemy [[Stance]] with its heavy attack version, leaving even bosses open for a [[Critical Attack]] within 2 or 3 hits. 

==Moveset==
The moveset mostly consists of slashes, with thrusts on every odd one-handed heavy attack, light rolling attack, every third dual wield attack, and dual wield backstep attacks. 

It's important to know when to use one-handed or two-handed light attacks, as the one-handed ones wide, horizontal swings makes catching evasive foes or hitting multiple ones easier, while the two-handed ones more vertical approach lets them hit slightly above the wielder and are much easier to use in close environments where other attacks might bounce off walls. 

Some straight swords have different standing heavy attacks: the [[Short Sword]] always makes thrusts instead of alternating between thrusts and slashes, the [[Broadsword]] and [[Cane Sword]] has only slashes on their heavy attacks (and their rolling light attack, which most notably also comes out slightly quicker than normal), the [[Warhawk's Talon]] that hits twice wit

weapons:Greatswords:missed.title: Greatswords
weapons:Greatswords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>{{for|the [[Colossal Swords|Colossal Sword]]|Greatsword}}[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Greatsword.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}. They boast high damage and reach at a minor cost of attack speed and mobility. 

With most of the one-handed regular attacks consisting of wide horizontal swings that cover a decent area in front of oneself, and the two-handed regular attacks being mostly vertical, as well as having [[Hyper Armor]] and dealing decent amounts of [[Poise]] Damage.

==Moveset==
The moveset mostly consists of slashes, with thrusts only on dual wield backstep attacks and certain greatswords' unique heavy attacks. 

It's important to know when to use one-handed or two-handed light attacks, as the one-handed ones wide, horizontal swings makes catching evasive foes or hitting multiple ones easier, while the two-handed ones more vertical approach lets them hit slightly above the wielder and are much easier to use in close environments where other attacks might bounce off walls. The heavy attacks have the inverse of this, with one-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks being overhead slams and two-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks are horisontal sweeps. 

Three greatswords have different two-handed light attacks: the [[Banished Knight's Greatsword]], [[Knight's Greatsword]], and [[Inseparable Sword]] have their light attacks alternate between horisontal and vertical slashes instead of the usual moveset's verticaly-only slashes. Some greatswords also have different heavy attacks: the [[Claymore]] and [[Greatsword of Damnation]] {{SOTE}} have thrusts for their one- and two-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks, and their two-handed rolling attacks and crouching attacks, the [[Flamberge]] has unique one-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks which are sweeps, the [[Marais Executioner's Sword]] has unique two-handed heavy attacks and unique heavy attacks which are

weapons:Colossal Swords:missed.title: Colossal Swords
weapons:Colossal Swords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Colossal Sword.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}. They have excellent reach and area coverage, along with high damage against both [[HP|health]] and [[poise]], at the cost of their high weight and slow attack speed. Its one-handed moveset's slow speed is at risk of getting parried; however, except for the [[Starscourge Greatsword]] and the two [[Greatsword of Radahn (Light)|Greatswords]] of [[Greatsword of Radahn (Lord)|Radahn]] the two-handed moveset is unparryable. 

Most Colossal Swords require a decent amount of investment into the [[Strength]] attribute in order to be wielded; however, it's possible to still wield a colossal sword with less strength than listed through two-handing, as two-handing increases the player's effective Strength by 50%.

==Moveset==
The Colossal Sword moveset notably doesn't notably change between the one-handed and two-handed versions. The moveset mostly consists of horisontal slashes, with overhead slams for heavy attacks, charged heavy attacks, and running heavy attacks, and thrusts for the crouching attack and the rolling attack. 

Two Colossal Swords, the [[Godslayer's Greatsword]] and the [[Fire Knight's Greatsword]] {{SOTE}} have slightly quicker, less staggering light attacks that also grant more points for the [[Winged Sword Insignias]]. Seven Colossal Swords have unique heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks, with the [[Zweihander]], Fire Knight's Greatsword {{SOTE}}, [[Troll Knight's Sword]], [[Moonrithyll's Knight Sword]] {{SOTE}}, and [[Ancient Meteoric Ore Greatsword]] {{SOTE}} have thrusting attacks, the [[Greatsword]] has sweeping attacks, and the [[Ruins Greatsword]] has extra follow-up gravitational eruptions after its charged heavy attacks. 

Three Colossal Swords, the [[Starscourge Greatsword]] and both [[Greatsword of Radahn (Lord)|Greatswords]] of [[Greatsword of Radahn (Light)|Radahn]] are paired weapons, and thus lac

weapons:Thrusting Swords:missed.title: Thrusting Swords
weapons:Thrusting Swords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Thrusting Sword.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}. 
They possess on average low weight, low stamina cost on attacking, quick attack speed and reasonable range at the cost of low damage against [[HP|health]], [[poise]] and the stamina of guarding opponents. Their subtle telegraphs at the start of most of their attack animations make it difficult for other players to react to, which turns them into excellent sidearms when dual-wielded alongside most other weapon types. Similar to [[Curved Swords]] their heavy attack charges can be canceled in favor of a quick feint-attack should the player press the [[Roll]] button while charging. They can also attack while guarding at the same time similar to [[Heavy Thrusting Swords]] and [[Spears]].

Their two-handed moveset is generally very similar to their one-handed one, increasing overall damage to poise and health at the cost of the perks gained from being used alongside other weapons and shields. 

Their dual-wielded moveset consists of flurries of several smaller, lower-damage attacks of varying numbers per button press, allowing one to inflict status ailments faster at the cost of committing to longer-lasting attack animations and delayed recovery from said attacks.

weapons:Heavy Thrusting Swords:missed.title: Heavy Thrusting Swords
weapons:Heavy Thrusting Swords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Heavy Thrusting Sword.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.
They have moderately quick attack speed and reasonable range as well as more damage against [[HP|health]] and [[poise]], and have a relatively subtle telegraphs at the start of most of their attack animations. However they are overall slower and have a higher stamina cost compared to the smaller [[Thrusting Swords]]. 

==Moveset==
All attacks are [[Pierce]] damage thrusts, except for the [[Standard]] damage slash of feint-attacks. The most notable attack is the running heavy attack, which is a short, quick dash followed by the thrust itself, giving the attack greater than usual reach. Their two-handed moveset is generally very similar to their one-handed one, increasing overall damage to health and poise. 

Similar to [[Curved Swords]] and [[Thrusting Swords]], their charged heavy attack can be canceled in favor of a quick feint-attack should the player press the [[Roll]] button while charging. They can also attack while guarding at the same time similar to smaller to their [[Thrusting Swords|smaller brethren]], [[Spears]], and [[Great Spears]]. 

Unusually, all infusable Heavy Thrusting Swords except for [[Queelign's Greatsword]] {{SOTE}} have certain unique heavy attacks: the [[Godskin Stitcher]] has double-hit charged heavy attacks with no penalty to [[Status Effects|Status Effect]] build-up or Stamina damage, the [[Great Épée]] has sweeping heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks with increased and decreased Stamina damage when one-handed and two-handed respectively, and the [[Sword Lance]] {{SOTE}} has Great Spear heavy attacks, charged heavy attacks, running heavy attacks, jumping heavy attacks, and guard counters (more specifically those of the [[Lance]]). The Sword Lance notably also lacks the feint-attack as Great Spears do not have those.

weapons:Curved Swords:missed.title: Curved Swords
weapons:Curved Swords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Curved Sword.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

They have swift, slashing attacks at the cost of slightly lower damage per hit, and are also one of a few Weapon Classes that can perform feint attacks. 

==Moveset==
The one- and two-handed movesets are largely identical, with the only real standout being that their charged heavy attacks hit twice and that dodging when charging a heavy attack cancels it to perform a feint attack, with a weak attack followed by a backwards flip. 

The dual-wield moveset is more notable, especially the dual-wield jumping attack and the dual-wield running attack, both of which hit four times each to give good synergy with multi-hit damage buffs like the [[Winged Sword Insignias]] and with inflicting [[Status Effects]] such as [[Blood Loss]].

Some Curved Swords have unique attacks, with the [[Scimitar]] and [[Shamshir]] having smoother light attacks that move the user a bit more forwards than usual for a bit more reach, the three shotels (the [[Shotel]], [[Nox Flowing Sword]], and [[Eclipse Shotel]]) ignore part of the Guarded Damage Negation of anything used to Guard against them and have slightly quicker charged heavy attacks that only hit once, the [[Beastman's Curved Sword]] has multi-hit jumping light attacks and jumping heavy attacks, and the [[Flowing Curved Sword]] and [[Dancing Blade of Ranah]] {{SOTE}} hit twice instead of once with heavy attacks and thrice instead of twice with charged heavy attacks. 

There are also some extra unique properties of several Curved Swords: the [[Mantis Blade]] extends during heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks to gain extra reach, the [[Wing of Astel]] sends out magical projectiles with heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks, and the [[Falx]] {{SOTE}}, [[Dancing Blade of Ranah]] {{SOTE}}, and [[Horned Warrior's Sword]] {{SOTE}} are paired weapons that use the dual-wield moveset instead of the tw

weapons:Curved Greatswords:missed.title: Curved Greatswords
weapons:Curved Greatswords:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Curved Greatsword.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

Almost all of their attacks are wide sweeps with decent amounts of [[Hyper Armor]] and [[Poise]] Damage. While they are technically a larger version of [[Curved Swords]], unlike them Curved Greatswords aren't capable of making feint attacks. 

They have some unique qualities, as the [[Onyx Lord's Greatsword]] deals 20% more damage to [[Gravity]]-aspected enemies and [[Freyja's Greatsword]] {{SOTE}} increases the damage dealt by [[Redmane]]-affiliated skills. 

==Moveset==
The moveset consists almost entirely of wide, sweeping slashes, with a a few slams mixed in for the jumping heavy attacks and some unique heavy attacks. 

One Curved Greatsword, the [[Zamor Curved Sword]], has unique, twirling light attacks that deal slightly more damage in exchamge for being ever so slightly slower. Two Curved Greatswords have unique two-handed heavy attacks, as the [[Omen Cleaver]] and [[Magma Wyrm's Scalesword]] have short-ranged guillotine chops for their two-handed heavy attacks, charged heavy attacks, and jumping heavy attacks. These deal slightly more damage to compensate for their much shorter reach than normal. 

While the [[Bloodhound's Fang]] doesn't technically have unique attacks it does deal substantially more damage than expected with its one- and two-handed jumping light attacks and jumping heavy attacks due to having higher Motion Values. 

==Ashes of War==
Curved Greatswords are considered as "large swords capable of slashing". Curiously and in spite of this, certain thrusting-based skills such as [[Skill: Glintstone Pebble|Glintstone Pebble]] and [[Skill: Aspects of the Crucible: Wings|Aspects of the Crucible: Wings]] can still be applied to them. As such, the infusable ones are compatible with the following Ashes of War: 
<section begin="Curved Greatswords AoWs"/><!--
-->

weapons:Katanas:missed.title: Katanas
weapons:Katanas:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Katana.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

They are a type of slightly curved sword that seem to have originated in the [[Land of Reeds]], though unlike Curved Swords they are still capable of making thrusting attacks. All of them (except for the [[Dragonscale Blade]]) inflict a [[Status Effect]] with their attacks by default, primarily [[Blood Loss]], and consist of far more unique weapons than infusable ones. 

==Moveset==
Most attacks are [[Slash]]es, with one-handed attacks being horisontal ones and two-handed attacks being mainly vertical but slightly tilted. Of the [[Pierce]] attacks, in the standard moveset these are the first one-handed heavy attack and charged heavy attack, the one- and two-handed running heavy attacks, and the second hit of the third attack in the dual wield attack chain. Katanas have a dual-wield moveset with staggered attacks, making it one of the slower dual-wield movesets for medium-sized weapons. 

For the unique attacks, the [[Nagakiba]] have Pierce attacks for its second one-handed heavy attack and charged heavy attack, and its two-handed heavy attack and charged heavy attacks, the [[Meteoric Ore Blade]] have entirely vertical one- and two-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks, and the [[Serpentbone Blade]] and [[Sword of Night]] {{SOTE}} have horisontal, double-hitting one- and two-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks. 

In addition to these weapons, the [[Wakizashi]] [[Daggers|dagger]] counts as a Katana when dual-wielding, being intended to serve as an off-hand companion to regular Katanas. 

==Ashes of War==
Katanas are considered as "medium swords capable of slashing and thrusting". As such, the infusable ones are compatible with the following Ashes of War: 
<section begin="Katanas AoWs"/><!--
-->

weapons:Twinblades:missed.title: Twinblades
weapons:Twinblades:missed.wikitext:
{{for|the weapon|Twinblade}}
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Twinblade.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

Though they have decent one-handed and dual-wielded moveset, Twinblades are more focused on their two-handed attacks which are often multi-hits, making them good for [[Status Effects]] and the [[Winged Sword Insignias]]. 

Prior to [[Game Version/1.07|Patch 1.07's]] nerf to dual-wielded Status Effect build-up, dual-wielded Twinblades used to inflict 280% total Status Effect build-up with the jumping dual-wield attack (160% total after the nerf). 

==Moveset==
Almost all Twinblade attacks are slashes, with all one-handed attacks aside from the running heavy attack (which hits two times) being single hits. For the two-handed moveset, many attacks hit twice (the first, second, and fourth attacks in the light attack chain, the first heavy attack, the running light and heavy attacks, the rolling attack, the jumping light attack, and the guard counter), and a few hit either three (the fifth attack in the light attack chain, and the second heavy attack) or four times (first and second charged heavy attacks).

The only [[Pierce]] attacks in the default moveset are the one-handed running light attack, backstep light attack, and the jumping heavy attack, and the two-handed backstep light attack and jumping heavy attack. 

There are a few unique attacks: the [[Godskin Peeler]] and [[Black Steel Twinblade]] {{SOTE}} have stabbing one- and two-handed heavy attacks, charged heavy attacks, and running heavy attacks that deal Pierce damage (only the second hit of the running heavy attacks), as does the final hit of all their two-handed light attacks, and [[Eleonora's Poleblade]] has spinning one- and two-handed heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks, hitting twice instead of once for the first one-handed heavy attack, four and three times instead of once for the one-handed charged heavy attacks, once instead of t

weapons:Great Katanas:missed.title: Great Katanas
weapons:Great Katanas:missed.wikitext:
{{for|the weapon|Great Katana}}
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Great Katana.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{SotE}}.

They are a larger, heavier variant of [[Katanas]], trading some speed for more power. Like that weapon class, Great Katanas originate in the [[Land of Reeds]] and inflict a [[Status Effect]] with their attacks, [[Blood Loss]] in all cases. 

==Moveset==
The moveset is heavily slash-focused, with thrusting attacks by default only being found on the one- and two-handed running light attacks, rolling attacks, and backstep attacks, the third attack in the one-handed light attack chain and the second attack in the two-handed light attack chain, the second hit of the second attack in the dual-wield attack chain, both hits of the running dual-wield attack, and the first hit in the dual-wield backstep attack. 

One unusual quality, especially as it isn't shared with Katanas, is that Great Katanas have the ability to make feint attacks, which is canceling a charged heavy attack by dodging during its charge-up period. 

For unique attacks, [[Rakshasa's Great Katana]] have thrusting heavy attacks, charged heavy attacks, and feint attacks (though the feint attack has the exact same slashing animation as the other Great Katanas). 

==Ashes of War==
Great Katanas are considered as "large swords capable of slashing and thrusting". As such, the infusable ones are compatible with the following [[Ashes of War]]:

weapons:Axes:missed.title: Axes
weapons:Axes:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Axe.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

A handful of axes in ''ELDEN RING'' are referred to as "hatchets", namely: the [[Forked Hatchet]], the [[Forked-Tongue Hatchet]] {{SOTE}}, the [[Hand Axe]], and the [[Icerind Hatchet]]. These axes possess faster attack swings on their light attacks and are much lighter than the other axes.

The Warped Axe additionally features increased [[Status Effects|Status Effect]] build-up from the [[Cold Affinity|Cold]], [[Poison Affinity|Poison]], and [[Blood Affinity|Blood Affinities]], on par with [[Greataxes]]. 

==Moveset==
The Axe moveset consists mostly of diagonal slashes, with a few overhead slams mixed in on the final light attack in a chain, the heavy attacks and charged heavy attacks, crouching attacks, rolling attack, running heavy attack. 

Three subgroups of Axes have unique light attacks: small Axes (the [[Forked Hatchet]], [[Forked-Tongue Hatchet]] {{SOTE}}, [[Hand Axe]], and [[Icerind Hatchet]]) have a slightly quicker light attack chain, double-headed Axes ([[Messmer Soldier's Axe]] {{SOTE}}, [[Ripple Blade]], and [[Warped Axe]]) use the other side of their blade to hit slightly quicker with each attack after the first one, and cleavers ([[Celebrant's Cleaver]] and [[Iron Cleaver]]) have more repititive light attacks with roughly the same speed as the regular ones. Of these subgroups, the double-headed Axes have unique heavy and charged heavy attacks due to the second attack of each hitting twice, and the cleavers add a small run-up before their heavy and charged heavy attacks. Furthermore, the [[Smithscript Axe]] {{SOTE}} is also thrown for its heavy, charged heavy, and jumping heavy attacks, and the [[Death Knight's Twin Axes]] {{SOTE}} is a [[Paired Weapon]], switching out the ability to two-hand it for an innate access to the dual-wield moveset. 

One large and notable downside of the Axe moveset is its dual-wielding att

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Golden horn of the Oracle Envoys. Profoundly weighty, its blows are sure to be felt.

|EN_line2= Originally an instrument, but one that cannot be sounded by a mere human. Or perhaps it is too early to sound the call.

|EN_line3= Unique Skill : Oracular Bubble

|EN_line4= Blow on the horn to release a magic bubble. The bubble floats gently through the air towards its target.


|JP_line1= 神託の使者たちの黄金の笛ずっしりと重く、打ち据える武器にもなる

|JP_line2= 本来はやはり楽器であるが人の身で、吹き鳴らすことはできないあるいは、まだその時は来ていない

|JP_line3= 専用戦技「信託のシャボン」

|JP_line4= 笛を吹き、魔力のシャボンを放つ戦技シャボンはゆっくりと飛びながら目標にふわふわと向かっていく

}}

weapons:Flails.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Weapon|title=Family Heads|image  = ER Icon weapon Family Heads.png|magic_guarded=36|fai_req=0|int_req=16|dex_req=18|str_req=8|int_scale=E|dex_scale=C|str_scale=E|guard_boost=20|holy_guarded=26|lightning_guarded=26|fire_guarded=26|physical_guarded=37|type=Flail|critical=100|holy_power=0|lightning_power=0|fire_power=0|magic_power=56|physical_power=87|sell_price=200|weight=5.5|fp_cost=25|skills=[[Unique Skill: Familial Rancor|Familial Rancor]]|attack_type=Strike|arc_req=0}}

'''Family Heads''' is a [[Weapons|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}.

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description
|EN_line1= Three bludgeoning copper heads attached to a handle by chains. Signature weapon of Necromancer Garris, the heretical sage.
|EN_line2= The heads were made to resemble those of his wife and two children.
|EN_line3= [[Unique Skill: Familial Rancor]]
|EN_line4= Gently rattle the copper heads to summon vengeful spirits that chase down foes. The anguish of a spouse and children invites accursed wrath.
|JP_line1= 赤銅製の首を鎖で繋いだ武器異端の賢者、死術師ガレスの得物
|JP_line2= その首は、妻と二人の子に似せて作られている
|JP_line3= 専用戦技「家族の怨霊」
|JP_line4= 赤銅の首をゆっくりと揺らしながら追いすがる怨霊たちを呼ぶ戦技妻子の苦悶が、祟り招くのだ
}}

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition==
Received upon defeating [[Necromancer Garris]], who is found behind an illusory wall inside the [[Sage's Cave]], located in [[Altus Plateau]].

==[[File:ER Main Menu Icon Messages.png|40px|Notes]] Notes==
* While the weapon itself mostly scales with [[Dexterity]], the skill actually scales with [[Intelligence]] and quite strongly at that in spite of the weapon's own E to D scaling for that [[Attributes|Attribute]].
* The [[Strength]] requirement is 8, which is the lowest the Attribute can go.
* As a [[Flails|Flail]], this weapon can't be parried. 
* Curiously enough, the skill does not consume [[Stamina]] upon casting, not even in combat.

'''Beastclaw Greathammer''' is a [[weapons|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= [[Great Hammers|Great hammer]] with a striking end modelled to resemble [[Cinquedea|five beastly claws]].

|EN_line2= The black nails protruding from golden fur are said to represent [[Serosh]], Lord of [[Beasts]], who went on to become [[Elden Lord|King]] [[Godfrey]]'s Regent.

|EN_line3= [[Unique Skill: Regal Beastclaw]]

|EN_line4= Slam the hammer into the ground, rending the land asunder with the armament's five bestial claws.


|JP_line1= 柄頭に五本の獣爪を配した大槌

|JP_line2= 黄金の鬣を纏う黒き爪は後にゴッドフレイ王の宰相となった獣王セローシュの象徴であったという

|JP_line3= 専用戦技「獣王の爪」

|JP_line4= 大槌を地面に叩きつけ五本の獣爪で、大地を大きく引き裂く戦技

}}

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition==

'''Axe of Godfrey''' is a [[weapons|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}.

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Weapon of [[Godfrey, First Elden Lord|Godfrey]], [[Elden Lord]]. It was broken in a battle fought as leader of the [[Tarnished]] during the [[Long March]].

|EN_line2= 
This weapon is symbolic of Godfrey's [[Serosh|vow]] to conduct himself as a lord, later becoming an emblem of the [[Golden Lineage|golden lineage]].

|EN_line3= In the days of the past, a [[Elden Lord Crown|crown]] was warranted with strength.

|EN_line4= [[Unique Skill: Regal Roar]]

|EN_line5= Let loose a mighty war cry, raising attack power, while sending out a shockwave that cannot be guarded against by stomping the ground. While active, strong attack becomes a lunging slash.


|JP_line1= エルデの王、ゴッドフレイの得物褪せ人を率いた長征の戦いにより、半壊している

|JP_line2= ゴッドフレイが王として振舞う誓いであり後には、黄金の一族の象徴ともなった武器

|JP_line3= かつて、力こそが王の故であった

|JP_line4= 専用戦技「王の雄叫び」

|JP_line5= 雄叫びを上げ、攻撃力を高める戦技大地を踏む衝撃波はガードすることができず効果中、強攻撃が踏み込み斬りとなる

}}

weapons:Colossal Weapons:missed.title: Colossal Weapons
weapons:Colossal Weapons:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Colossal Weapon.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

==Subtypes==
Colossal Weapons can be divided into two subtypes: axe-type Colossal Weapons with a base [[Stance]] damage of 6 and with [[Standard]] as their primary [[Physical]] damage type, and hammer-type Colossal Weapons with a base Stance damage of 7.5 and with [[Strike]] as their primary Physical damage type. This means that hammer-type Colossal Weapons have the highest Stance damage among all weapons while axe-type Colossal Weapons have Stance damage on par with [[Colossal Swords]] and [[Flails]], and are outmatched by [[Hammers]] (base 6.5 Stance damage), [[Great Hammers]] (base 7 Stance damage), and the aforementioned hammer-type Colossal Weapons. 

List of axe-type Colossal Weapons: [[Axe of Godfrey]], [[Dragon Greatclaw]], [[Duelist Greataxe]], [[Ghiza's Wheel]], [[Golem's Halberd]], and [[Rotten Greataxe]].  

List of hammer-type Colossal Weapons: [[Anvil Hammer]] {{SOTE}}, [[Bloodfiend's Arm]] {{SOTE}}, [[Devonia's Hammer]] {{SOTE}}, [[Envoy's Greathorn]], [[Fallingstar Beast Jaw]], [[Gazing Finger]] {{SOTE}}, [[Giant-Crusher]], [[Great Club]], [[Prelate's Inferno Crozier]], [[Rotten Staff]], [[Shadow Sunflower Blossom]] {{SOTE}}, [[Staff of the Avatar]], [[Troll's Hammer]], and [[Watchdog's Staff]]. 

==Ashes of War==
Somewhat notably for Ash of War-compatibility, the [[Golem's Halberd]] has [[Skill: Charge Forth|Charge Forth]] as its innate skill but cannot have [[Ash of War: Charge Forth]] applied to it, leaving that skill only compatible with [[Standard Affinity]]. 

Colossal Weapons are considered as "colossal axes and hammers". As such, the infusable ones are compatible with the following Ashes of War:

-->

| colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color: black; color: white;" |'''Ashes o

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description
| EN_line1 = Spear whittled from the weapon wielded by [[Ancient Dragon Gransax|Gransax]]. One of the [[Legendary Armaments (Achievement/Trophy)|legendary armaments]].
| EN_line2 = A great [[ancient dragon]], Gransax once rained calamity upon the [[Leyndell, Royal Capital|Royal Capital]] - the only time in historical record that Leyndell's walls have fallen. This marked the dawn of the [[Routing of the Ancient Dragons|war against the ancient dragons]].
| EN_line3 = [[Unique Skill: Ancient Lightning Spear]]
| EN_line4 = Imbue the armament with the ancient dragons' [[red lightning]], then throw it as a spear. Can be charged to increase its power.
| JP_line1 = 大古竜グランサクスの得物から削り出した槍「伝説の武器」のひとつ
| JP_line2 = 大古竜の襲来は、ローデイルの城壁が破れた歴史上唯一の事例であり続く古竜たちとの戦いの端緒でもあった
| JP_line3 = 専用戦技「古竜の雷」
| JP_line4 = 古竜の武器たる赤い雷を武器に纏いそれを、槍となして投擲する戦技タメ使用で強化される
}}

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description
|EN=Greatstaff of Jori, elder inquisitor. The tip bristles with golden barbs symbolic of the inquisition's torture, allowing one to wield the staff as a greatspear.

Attacks deal holy damage, and potency increases with faith.

|JP= 
責問の古老、イオリの大杖 先端部に責問の象徴たる黄金の逆棘があり 大槍として振るうことができる

聖属性の攻撃力を持ち その威力は信仰により高められる
}}

'''Banished Knight's Halberd''' is a [[weapons|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}.

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= A finely-crafted halberd intricately engraved with an ancient motif. Given to knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes.

|EN_line2= Most of these knights were sent to the fringes, where they were forced to start anew with only despair for company.

|JP_line1= 古い意匠が刻まれた、上質な斧槍何らかの理由、あるいは咎で故郷を失くした騎士たちに与えられた

|JP_line2= 彼らの多くは、辺境の地へと送られ失意と共に、その地に根付いたという

}}

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition==
* +8 version dropped by Edgar.
* Dropped by [[Banished Knight]] enemies that wield it. One can be found near the Rampart Tower [[Sites of Grace|Site of Grace]] in [[Stormveil Castle]].

'''Commander's Standard''' is a [[weapons|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}.

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= A beaten red battle standard is furled around this time-worn halberd.

|EN_line2= Even after his lord was fled, Commander O'Neil continued to brandish this flag in the devastation of the rot-eaten field of battle, the sole veteran who remembers this battle with pride.

|EN_line3= Unique Skill : Rallying Standard

|EN_line4= Hoist the war banner aloft and give a rallying command. Raises attack power and defense for self and nearby allies.

|JP_line1= 古めかしい斧槍にボロボロの赤い軍旗を巻き付けた得物

|JP_line2= もはや主の姿無き、腐敗した戦場跡でそれでも、宿将オニールが掲げ続けた旗老人は只一人、あの戦を誇っていた

|JP_line3= 専用戦技「軍旗の下に」

|JP_line4= 軍旗を掲げ、勇ましく号令をかける戦技自らと周囲の味方の、攻撃力と防御力を高める

}}

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition==
Received upon defeating Commander O'Neil in Aeonia Swamp located in Caelid.

A viciously keen and slender scythe made for combat. The obsidian edge relies not upon trickery to fulfil its violent purpose—through skill alone the wielder can rip and rend through foes, sowing blossoms of red upon the battlefield. |JP_Name=黒曜のラーミナ |JP_Source=[[1.12]] |JP=純血騎士アンスバッハの得物
研ぎ澄まされた細身の戦鎌 黒曜石の刃に小細工はなく
ただ、卓越した剣技により
敵手を切り裂き、血の花を咲かせる |CN_Name= |CN_Source=[[1.12]] |CN= |DE_Name= |DE_Source=[[1.12]] |DE= |FR_Name= |FR_Source=[[1.12]] |FR= |IT_Name= |IT_Source=[[1.12]] |IT= |KR_Name= |KR_Source=[[1.12]] |KR= |PL_Name= |PL_Source=[[1.12]] |PL= |BR_Name= |BR_Source=[[1.12]] |BR= |RU_Name= |RU_Source=[[1.12]] |RU= |ES_Name= |ES_Source=[[1.12]] |ES= |TH_Name= |TH_Source=[[1.12]] |TH= |TW_Name= |TW_Source=[

Perhaps that was a curse of their kind. |JP= 火の巨人の、燃えるような赤髪を編み込み束ねた太鞭 巨人たちは、皆一様に赤髪でありラダゴンは、自らの赤髪に絶望したというそれは巨人の呪いだったろうか }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Trade:''' [[Finger Reader Enia]] * Trade the [[Remembrance of the Fire Giant]], gained by defeating [[Fire Giant]], with [[Finger Reader Enia]]. ** Alternatively, use one of the [[Wandering Mausoleums]] to duplicate one (can only obtain one per playthrough using either method). ==[[File:ER Main Menu Icon Messages.png|40px|Notes]] Notes== * [[Unique Skill: Flame Dance|Flame Dance]] imbues the Giant's Red Braid with fire as the user swings it around. This work of art is handed down through the generations of the illustrious [[House Hoslow]]. High dexterity is required to wield this weapon, but it excels at inflicting devastating blood loss. "The tale of House Hoslow is told in blood." |JP= 鋭い刃の花弁を、鎖のように連ねた金属の鞭名門ホスローに伝わる、工芸品のような得物 高い技量が必要だが、対手に大量の出血を強いる「ホスローは血潮で物語る }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Guaranteed Drop:''' [[Mountaintops of the Giants]] * Dropped by [[Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood]] after invading him in the Mountaintops of the Giants for the final contract from [[Tanith, Volcano Manor Proprietress]]. ==[[File:ER Main Menu Icon Messages.png|40px|Notes]] Notes== * The damage formula when inflicting [[Blood Loss]] on a non-[[Blood Affinity|Blood-in

(Yōganben no Shokudai) | image = ER Icon weapon Magma Whip Candlestick.png | type = Whip | attack_type = Strike | skills = [[Unique Skill: Sea of Magma|Sea of Magma]] | fp_cost = 5 ( -5) | weight = 2.5 | buy_price = | sell_price = 200 | physical_power = 72 | magic_power = 0 | fire_power = 72 | lightning_power = 0 | holy_power = 0 | critical = 100 | physical_guarded = 21.0 | magic_guarded = 10.0 | fire_guarded = 36.0 | lightning_guarded = 10.0 | holy_guarded = 10.0 | guard_boost = 15 | range = | sorcery_scaling = | incant_scaling = | str_scale = E | dex_scale = D | int_scale = - | fai_scale = D | arc_scale = - | str_req = 8 | dex_req = 16 | int_req = 0 | fai_req = 18 | arc_req = 0 | effects = }} The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a [[Melee Armaments|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}. ==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description== {{Description |EN= This ritual implement is a three pronged candlestick with solid flames formed of the magma of Mt. Gelmir. When wielded as a weapon, the flames become supple whips of lava. |JP= 溶岩で炎姿を象った三又の燭台
ゲルミアの溶岩を用いた呪具 それを振るう時だけ
炎姿は、しなる溶岩の鞭となる }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Quest Reward:''' [[Patches]] * The reward from Patches in [[Volcano Manor]] after defeating [[Great Horned Tragoth]]. ** He won't give it to the player the first time he is spoken too. Instead, the area has to be reloaded in some way (either via teleporting or by exiting and re-entering the game) and he can be spoken to again to gain the weapon. {{Navbox Melee Armaments}} [[ru:Магмовый подсвечник]]

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= A weapon built for striking with the fists, made of rough leather inlaid with iron rivets.

|EN_line2= Fist weapons come in pairs, and two-handling this weapon will equip it to both hands.

|JP_line1= 厚手の革に鉄鋲を埋め込んだ拳で殴りつけるための武器

|JP_line2= 拳武器は二つ一組であり両手持ちすると、左右両拳に装備される

}}

weapons:Fists.wikitext:
{{Infobox Weapon | title=Cipher Pata | japanese = 秘文字のパタ
(Himoji no Pata) | image= ER Icon weapon Cipher Pata.png | type = Fist | attack_type = Slash/Pierce | skills = [[Unblockable Blade]] | fp_cost = 17 | weight = 0 | buy_price = | sell_price = 500 | physical_power = 0 | magic_power = 0 | fire_power = 0 | lightning_power = 0 | holy_power = 85 | critical = 100 | physical_guarded =9 | magic_guarded =14 | fire_guarded =14 | lightning_guarded =14 | holy_guarded =61 | guard_boost =19 | range = | sorcery_scaling = | incant_scaling = | str_scale = - | dex_scale = - | int_scale = - | fai_scale = C | arc_scale = - | str_req = 0 | dex_req = 0 | int_req = 0 | fai_req = 30 | arc_req = 0 | effects = }} '''Cipher Pata''' is a [[weapons|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}. ==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description== {{Description |EN= One of the weapons originating from the [[Two Fingers]]. A formless sequence of ciphers comprise its blade, and as such no [[Shields|shield]] can repel it. Deals [[holy]] damage. The furtive inscription appears to hang in the air; the language of light spoken by the Two Fingers. |JP= 二本指に由来する武器のひとつ実体のない秘文字の連なりを刃とし聖属性の攻撃力を持ち、盾に弾かれない 秘文字とは、中空に書き記される二本指の光の言葉である }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Loot:''' [[Roundtable Hold]] * Jump over the balcony rail in the Roundtable Hold, it will be in the rooms to the left. ==[[File:ER Main Menu Icon Blacksmith.png|40px|link=Upgrading]] Upgrading== {|class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" style="text-align:center;" !rowspan="2"|Level !colspan="6"|Attack Power !colspan="5"|Attribute Scaling !colspan="6"|Guarded Damage Negation |- |Physical |Magic |Fire |Lightning |Holy |Critical !Str !Dex !Int !Fai !Arc |Physical |Magic |Fire |Lightning

weapons:Fists.title: Clinging Bone
weapons:Fists.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Weapon|title=Clinging Bone|image  = ER Icon weapon Clinging Bone.png|magic_guarded=36|fai_req=0|int_req=0|dex_req=22|str_req=8|arc_scale=D|dex_scale=C|str_scale=E|guard_boost=21|holy_guarded=17|lightning_guarded=17|fire_guarded=17|physical_guarded=31|type=Fist|critical=100|holy_power=0|lightning_power=0|fire_power=0|magic_power=24|physical_power=80|sell_price=200|weight=3.0|fp_cost=14|skills=[[Ash of War: Lifesteal Fist|Lifesteal Fist]]|attack_type=Slash/Pierce|arc_req=16}}
'''Clinging Bone''' is a [[Weapons|melee armament]] found in ''[[Elden Ring (game)|Elden Ring]]''.

==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Horrific weapon made of a hardened skeletal arm. Wielded by Ensha of the Royal Remains.

|EN_line2= Fitted by placing one's hands into the fists' grips until they dig in. "O clinging creature. A king relinquishes not the hand.

|EN_line3= Skill: Lifesteal Fist

|EN_line4= Skill that demonstrate mastery of the art of controlling vital energies. A slow, controlled punch with an energy-infused fist that renders foes unconcious and steals their HP. Only effective against foes of human build.


|JP_line1= 人骨の腕を、硬く加工した異様な武器王骸のエンシャの得物

|JP_line2= 食い込むほどに、拳を握らせて装備する縋り付く者よ。王はその手を離さない

|JP_line3= 戦技「命奪拳」

|JP_line4= 気を極めた達人の戦技。人の類にしか効果がないゆっくりと気を纏った拳を突き出し触れた者を昏倒させ、ＨＰを奪う

}}

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition==
Received upon defeating [[Ensha of the Royal Remains]].

{{Navbox Melee Armaments}}

[[ru:Цепкая кость]]

weapons:Claws:missed.title: Claws
weapons:Claws:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Claw.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[weapons#Melee|melee armament]] in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
Claws are hand-held weapons consisting of one or more blades extending from the knuckles of the wielder. They are similar to [[Fists]] in terms of their attack patterns and range, though they scale primarily with [[Dexterity]] rather than [[Strength]]. Though they are shorter than most blades (ranging between that of [[Daggers]] and [[Straight Swords]]) and deal modest damage per hit, Claws excel at delivering rapid-fire, close-range slashing attacks that rend flesh easily. With the exception of the Venomous Fang, all Claws feature innate [[Blood Loss|Bleed]] Damage.

Each Claw comes in a set of two, with the Tarnished wielding one on each hand when equipping a Claw "two-handed" without taking up both hand slots. When equipped one-handed, the Tarnished will hold one claw in one hand while the other is left tied to their belt.

== Pros/Cons ==
* + Lightweight
* + Fast/high DPS
* + Can proc [[Blood Loss]] and other [[Status Effects|status effects]] quickly (if applied)
* + Paired Weapons - can be wielded in both hands with only one slot
* - Short Reach
* - Only four kinds available/cannot be farmed within the same playthrough (I.E. if a player wants to dual-wield two sets of the same type of Claws, they will have to wait until NG+ to acquire another)
* - Few [[Ashes of War]] available

==Ashes of War==
Claws are considered as "small claws". As such, the infusable ones are compatible with the following Ashes of War:

-->

weapons:Light Bows:missed.title: Light Bows
weapons:Light Bows:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Light Bow.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Ranged Weapons|ranged weapons]] in {{ER}}. Light Bows, as their name implies, are the lightest out of the Bow sub-classes, and corresponding have the lowest [[Attack Power]] and the shortest range, with an average range value of 29 (most other ranged weapons have a range value of 50; [[Crossbows]] have an average range value of 42). However, Light Bows fire faster than normal bows, and wielders can fire [[Ammunition|arrows]] immediately out of a sprint, roll, or jump, or during a jump, enabling them to have a more aggressive playstyle. In addition, [[Torrent]] will not slow down when the player uses a Light Bow while riding him. Light Bows only accept arrows as their ammunition.

The ability to fire immediately both during and after a jump creates a swift one-two combo where one can jump to reposition while firing an arrow and then fire an arrow immediately upon landing to cause damage quicker. 

Light Bows deal [[Pierce]] damage with their arrows.  

==Ashes of War==
Light Bows have the largest number of compatible Ashes of War among the bows (admittedly still a small number). All of them are locked to [[Standard Affinity]], regardless of the [[Whetblades]] that have been acquired. 

The following Ashes of War are compatible with infusable Light Bows:

weapons:Bows:missed.title: Bows
weapons:Bows:missed.wikitext:
[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Bow.png|thumb]] '''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Ranged Weapons|ranged weapons]] in {{ER}}. Bows, in a lot of ways, are the most average out of the Bow sub-classes. They have average weight, moderate [[Attack Power]], and a normal firing rate. Bows can be fired mid-jump and the wielder can move around while readying an [[Ammunition|arrow]], but arrows cannot be fired out of a roll or a sprint like they can while using a [[Light Bows|Light Bow]]. Bows can be fired while riding on [[Torrent]], however, the steed will slow down while firing. Bows only accept arrows as their ammunition. The [[Black Bow]] and [[Ansbach's Longbow]] {{SOTE}} have the same moveset as Light Bows while still being classified as Bows. Bows deal [[Pierce]] damage with their arrows. ==Ashes of War== Bows, like all ranged weapons, have a very small number of compatible Ashes of War. All of them are locked to [[Standard Affinity]], regardless of the [[Whetblades]] that have been acquired. The following Ashes of War are compatible with infusable Bows:

weapons:Crossbows:missed.title: Crossbows
weapons:Crossbows:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Crossbow.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Ranged Weapons|ranged weapons]] in {{ER}}. Crossbows have average-to-good [[Attack Power]], and unlike the other ranged weapons (e.g. [[Bows]] and [[Ballistas]]), Crossbows can be fired without having to two-hand them, enabling for potential combos and more aggressive gameplay with the player's main weapon. However, Crossbows have multiple drawbacks: Crossbows generally have less range than the other ranged weapon classes, with an average range value of 42 (most other ranged weapons have an average range value of 50, with [[Light Bows]] and their 29 range being the only shorter one), Crossbows must be loaded with a [[Bolt]] before every shot, hindering their rate of fire, and just like with [[Ballistas]] Crossbows do not scale with any stat, meaning that their potential damage output is flat. Crossbows only accept bolts as their ammunition. 

Most Crossbows are single-shot weapons, though the [[Pulley Crossbow]] fires three bolts in a row and the [[Spread Crossbow]] fires ten bolts in a three row spread. 

Crossbows deal [[Pierce]] damage through their bolts. 

==Ashes of War==
Crossbows are not compatible with any Ashes of War. 

{{clear}}

weapons:Ballistas:missed.title: Ballistas
weapons:Ballistas:missed.wikitext:
{{for|the stationary weapon|Ballista}} [[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Ballista.png|thumb]] '''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Ranged Weapons|ranged weapons]] in {{ER}}. They possess immense [[Attack Power]] and [[Stamina]] damage, making them well-fitting for dealing damage at long ranges and breaking enemy guards. To counter this, however, Ballistas do not scale with any stat, similar to [[Crossbows]], and push the user backwards slightly when fire. In addition, Ballistas have an incredibly long reload time after each shot which locks the user in place during the entire animation, forcing its wielders to use them from a fair distance from the opponents, lest they risk being punished (though it is possible to cancel this animation by falling down a drop during it, as this is not a true reload i.e. it is not needed to fire again). Ballistas cannot be fired mid-jump nor can they be fired when riding on [[Torrent]]; instead the user will swing the Ballista like it's a normal weapon. Ballistas only accept [[Ammunition|Greatbolts]] as their ammunition. Ballistas deal [[Pierce]] damage through their Greatbolts. ==Ashes of War== Ballistas are not compatible with any Ashes of War. {{clear}} ==List of Ballistas== |-|Gallery= {| class = " mw-collapsible wikitable" style= " width:100%" |- |[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Ballista.png|25px|link=Ballistas]] [[Ballistas]] |- | ER Icon weapon Hand Ballista.png|[[Hand Ballista]]|link=Hand Ballista ER Icon weapon Jar Cannon.png|[[Jar Cannon]]|link=Jar Cannon ER Icon Weapon Rabbath's Cannon.png|[[Rabbath's Cannon]]

== Description ==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Staff of the Academy of Raya Lucaria, embedded with a turquoise glintstone.

|EN_line2= Only a recognized sorcerer is permitted to wield this staff.

|JP_line1= 青緑の輝石が埋め込まれた杖レアルカリアの学院の魔術杖

'''Guaranteed drop:''' [[Liurnia of the Lakes]]
* Dropped by [[Thops]], either by killing him in the [[Church of Irith]] or upon finishing his questline, inside the [[Academy of Raya Lucaria]] just before the Schoolhouse Classroom site of grace.
'''Drop:''' [[Liurnia of the Lakes]]/[[Caelid]]/[[Mt. Gelmir]]
* 2% drop chance from [[Glintstone Sorcerer]]s (excluding the Lazuli variant).
** These Sorcerers are common enemies throughout [[Academy of Raya Lucaria]], with 7 in particular being found on the way to the [[Red Wolf of Radagon]] from the [[Schoolhouse Classroom]] Site of Grace, along with a Lazuli Sorcerer and a [[Living Jar Warrior]]. 
** Two Sorcerers can be found just next to the [[Primeval Sorc

'''Albinauric Staff''' is a [[Weapons|catalyst]] found in ''[[Elden Ring (game)|Elden Ring]]''.

== Description ==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Short staff with a blue glintstone embedded. Wielded by the Albinaurics of old.

|EN_line2= The Albinaurics harbor a secret; they cast sorcery using their innate arcaneness.

|JP_line1= 青い輝石が埋め込まれた、短い杖古いしろがね人たちの輝石杖

|JP_line2= しろがね人には、秘密がある彼らは、魔術を神秘で振るうのだ

}}

==Acquisition==
'''Loot:''' [[Mt. Gelmir]]
* Found together with the [[Albinauric Mask]] in the same room as an [[Omenkiller]], located on the third floor of the same building as the [[Guest Hall]] Site of Grace, located in [[Volcano Manor]].
** This room is accessed by jumping onto a balcony from the roof of the building. The balcony is located to the right of the building when looking towards the main entrance on the second floor. 

==Notes==
* An interesting Staff, as it's the only one that scales with [[Arcane]] in the base game ({{SotE}} also adds the [[Maternal Staff]]). Despite it's seemingly Arcane-leaning Scaling, it actually performs best on a split Intelligence/Arcane build (325 Sorcery Scaling for a 45/45 hybrid build vs 297 Scaling for a pure 80 Arcane build). 
* Note that only the Intelligence portion of the Sorcery Scaling is used when casting [[Scholar's Armament]], meaning that this Staff is rather unsuitable for that spell. 
* Increases the [[Blood Loss]] build-up inflicted by Sorceries, depending on how upgraded the Albinauric Staff is and t

weapons:Glintstone Staves:missed.title: Staves
weapons:Glintstone Staves:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Staff.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Catalysts|catalyst]] in {{ER}}. They function as catalysts for casting [[Sorceries]], and are required for doing so.

They generally scale with [[Intelligence]], but there are also those that scale with a mixture of that stat and either [[Faith]] ([[Gelmir Glintstone Staff]], [[Prince of Death's Staff]], and [[Staff of the Great Beyond]] {{SOTE}}) or [[Arcane]] ([[Albinauric Staff]] and [[Maternal Staff]] {{SOTE}}), or entirely with Faith ([[Staff of the Guilty]]). 

Staves have a base [[Stance]] damage of 3 for their melee attacks. 

==Moveset==
Staves will actually perform a melee attack when hitting the heavy attack button, but due to their low [[Attack Power]] this will usually not be very useful. 

Sorceries are cast by using a light attack when two-handed or held in the right hand, and by blocking when held in the left hand. 

==Ashes of War==
Staves only have one compatible Ash of War, the [[Ash of War: Spinning Weapon]], though they aren't a very fitting match for that one's skill. 

{{clear}}

'''Dragon Communion Seal''' is a [[Sacred Seals|sacred seal]] in {{ER}}. It boosts the power of [[Dragon Communion Incantations]].

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Description.png|50px|Description]] Description ==
{{Description

|EN= Formless drakeblood seal with a [[Dragon Communion|dragon communion]] crest. Enhances [[Dragon Communion Incantations|Dragon Communion incantations]].

The sacrificial devouring of the heart gives power. Indeed, Dragon Communion is too primal in nature for the term "incantation" to be appropriate. This seal scales incantations with one's [[Arcane|arcane]] attribute.

|JP= 三本指の巫女が授ける、幻影の聖印狂い火の祈祷を強化する

それは、狂い火の王たる証であり筋力、技量、知力、信仰そのすべてが祈祷を補正する

}}


==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|50px|Acquisition]] Acquisition==
Dropped by a spectral [[Banished Knight]] located in the [[Fringefolk Hero's Grave]] in [[Limgrave]]. To find it, start at the Stranded Graveyard Site of Grace and head down the first two ramps in the Grave, then head right in the split to go up another two ramps. The Knight is inside a small room at the end of the ramp and is quite tough for the early game. The Chariot in the dungeon can be used to help kill it, though that comes at considerable risk to the player as well. 

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effects==
Used to cast [[Incantations]]. Boosts the effectiveness of Dragon Communion Incantations by 15%.

==Description==
{{Description
|EN=Small wooden roundshield that has been reinforced with metal. It is light and easy to handle, but cannot offer the damage negation of a true metal shield.
The design is said to represent the stars of the night sky, portending fate.

|JP= 金属補強された、木製の小円盾軽く扱いやすいが、カット率は金属盾に劣る

その意匠は、夜空の星、そして運命を示すという
}}

'''Buckler''' is a [[Shields|shield]] in {{ER}}.

==Description==
{{Description

|EN= A small metal roundshield. The bump in the center is designed to [[Skill: Buckler Parry|parry]].

A well-timed parry deflects an enemy's attack, breaking their [[stance]] and leaving them open to a [[Critical Attack|critical hit]]. Though risk is inherent, this shield favors aggression.

|JP= 小さな円型の金属盾中央に大きな膨らみがあり、パリィに適する

タイミングよく攻撃を受け流すことで敵の体勢を崩し、致命の一撃を狙っていくリスクを負って攻める盾

}}

==Acquisition==
'''Equipped:'''
* One is part of the starting gear for the [[Bandit]] class.
'''Purchase:''' [[Stormveil Castle]]
* One is sold for {{icon|runes}} 1,500 [[Runes]] by [[Gatekeeper Gostoc]]. 

==Notes==
* The innate skill, [[Skill: Buckler Parry|Buckler Parry]], is effectively a semi-unique version of the regular [[Skill: Parry]] that the user recovers slightly faster from. 
** This only applies to the innate skill, so any other shield/parry skill will recovers as quickly with this skill as with any other compatible shield. 
** Though the skill ends up being slightly outmatched by [[Skill: Carian Retaliation]] it's still always the parry skill with the shortest total animation, if not by much.

weapons:Small Shields:missed.title: Small Shields
weapons:Small Shields:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Small Shield.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Shields|shield]] in {{ER}}.

Despite being shields, they are rather unsuited for blocking in all but emergency situations. Instead, their light weight combined with being infusable and sometimes providing bonuses to Status Effect resistances can make them good tools to have in the off-hand. 

Small shields on average also have the most [[parrying]] frames of all the shields and have the least amount of recovery frames after performing said action, the [[Buckler]] in particular being the best shield for parrying due to having a 5 frame active parry window and 12 frames of recovery.

'''Albinauric Shield''' is a [[shields|shield]] found in {{ER}}.

==Description==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Tall oval shield made of metal carried by young Albinaurics.

|EN_line2= The ornamentation represents the primordial drop of dew from which they are said to have been created. Boasts exceptional magic damage negation. The Albinaurics' most formidable foes were sorcerers, after all.

|JP_line1= 縦に長い、楕円の金属盾若きしろがね人たちの得物

|JP_line2= その紋様は、彼らの原初たる雫を示し魔力ダメージのカット率に優れるしろがね人の敵は、魔術師だった

}}

==Acquisition==
'''Drop:''' [[Liurnia of the Lakes]] / [[Consecrated Snowfield]]
* 2% drop chance from curved sword- or axe-wielding [[Second-Generation Albinauric]] enemies. 
** Three shield-wielders can be found on a sunken church/trio of buildings northwest of the [[Academy Gate Town (site of grace)|Academy Gate Town]] Site of Grace and southwest of the icon for [[Academy Gate Town]] itself. 
** Note that the blood red Albinarics in [[Mohgwyn Dynasty Mausoleum]] don't drop this shield. 

==Trivia==
* [[Game Version/1.14|Patch 1.14]] increased the Guard Boost from 51 to 56. 
* [[Game Version/1.03|Patch 1.03]] increased the Magic block from 61%

'''Banished Knight's Shield''' is a [[shields|shield]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
{{Description

|EN_line1= A tall medium-sized metal shield. Given to knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes.

|EN_line2= Most of these knights were sent to the fringes, where they were forced to start anew with only despair for company.

|JP_line1= 縦に長さのある、金属製の中盾何らかの理由、あるいは咎で故郷を失くした騎士たちに与えられた

|JP_line2= 彼らの多くは、辺境の地へと送られ失意と共に、その地に根付いたという

}}

==Acquisition==
'''Drop:''' [[Stormveil Castle]] / [[Cathedral of Dragon Communion]] <sup>([[Caelid]])</sup> / [[Castle Sol]] <sup>([[Mountaintops of the Giants]])</sup> / [[Crumbling Farum Azula]]
* 4% drop chance from shield-wielding [[Banished Knight]].
** The Knight at the entrance of the Cathedral of Dragon Communion is an easy target when farming for this item. 
** The Banished Knights in [[Fringefolk Hero's Grave]] do not drop this item. 

==Notes==
* This is the second strongest of the [[Medium Shields]] overall, requiring 2 less points in [[Strength]] to wield than the [[Brass Shield]] and weighting 1 points less than it in exchange

weapons:Medium Shields:missed.title: Medium Shields
weapons:Medium Shields:missed.wikitext:
<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Medium Shield.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Shields|shield]] in {{ER}}.

Compared to [[Small Shields]], Medium Shields are far more suited for blocking attacks as they have higher [[Guard Boost]] and [[Damage Negation|Damage Negations]] on average. 

On the flipside, Medium Shields weigh more and are worse at [[parrying]] than Small Shields, as they possess less active frames on their parries and have more recovery time.

There are four (but essentially three) types of shields in the game: wooden/leather, heater, and kite. Wooden and leather shields always have the lowest possible stat requirements to wield it (8 [[Strength]]) and are the lightest of all Medium Shields at the cost of lacking 100% Physical Damage Negation and the lowest Guard Boost. Heater shields need more Strength to wield but have 100% Damage Negation, middling weight and average Guard Boost. Kite shields have all the benefits of heather shields on top of the higher Guard Boost, but need more Strength wield and weigh more.

==Ashes of War==

<noinclude>[[File:ER Custom Sub-Menu Icon Greatshield.png|thumb]]
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' are a type of [[Weapons#Shields|shield]] in {{ER}}. 

Greatshields are the heaviest types of shields in the game, and cannot prefer any sort of [[Parrying|parry]]; instead they usually have [[Shield Bash]].

To make up for this, when compared to [[Small Shields]] and [[Medium Shields]], Greatshields have high [[Guard Boost]], high [[Damage Negation]]s and the highest [[Attack Power]] (though this doesn't matter as much since the main purpose of Greatshields is to block).

'''Beast-Repellent Torch''' is a [[weapons|melee armament]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
{{Description

|EN_line1= A torch which burns oil infused with special incense. The aroma pacifies wild beasts.

|EN_line2= Torches such as these were used to keep unwelcome beasts away from treasure troves hidden in caves.

|EN_line3= Skill : Torch Attack

|EN_line4= Thrust torch forward to set foes ablaze. This skill is especially effective against foes who are weak to fire.

|JP_line1= 脂と共に特殊な香料を燃やす松明その香りは、獣の戦意を減退させる

|JP_line2= 洞窟に宝物を隠した者たちが住み着いた獣を退けるために用いるという

|JP_line3= 戦技「松明攻撃」

|JP_line4= 松明の炎を前方に突き出し、敵を焼く戦技火に弱い敵には有効である

}}

==Acquisition==
'''Purchase:''' [[Dragonbarrow]]
* 1x sold for {{icon|runes}} 1,200 [[Runes]] by the [[Isolated Merchant (Dragonbarrow)|Isolated Merchant]] at the [[Isolated Merchant's Shack (Dragonbarrow)|Isolated Merchant's Shack]] in north-west Dragonbarrow, [[Caelid]].

==Notes==
* The [[Fire]] Attack Power scales with nothing. 
* Pacifies nearby [[Basilisk]]s, [[Bear]]s, [[Boar]]s, [[Dog]]s, [[Giant Bat]]s, [[Giant Crow]]s, [[Giant Dog]]s, [[Giant Rat]]s, [[Ram]]

talismans.title: Outer God Heirloom
talismans.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Item
| title       = Outer God Heirloom
| image       = ER_Icon_Talisman_Outer_God_Heirloom.png
| type        = Talisman
| item_effect = Raises arcane by 5
| weight      = 0.6
| sell_price  = 1,000
| obtained    = [[Prospect Town]]
}}
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a [[Talismans (Shadow of the Erdtree)|Talisman]] in {{SotE}}.

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Description.png|50px|Description]] Description ==
{{Description
|EN=
A talisman engraved with the lore of an [[Outer God|outer god]]. Raises arcane.

The clan, who lost everything in the great fires, peered upon the corpse of their ancestor, normally an act of sanctity, and saw in its shadow a twisted deity.

The clan had suffered such torment that the horrible thing was taken as an object of worship.
}}

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|50px|Acquisition]] Acquisition==
'''<u>Loot:</u>''' [[Prospect Town]] <sup>([[Gravesite Plain]])</sup>
* It can be found inside a chest inside a small building.

==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effect==
Raises [[Arcane]] by 5.

==See Also==
* [[Starscourge Heirloom]], a Heirloom talisman that raises [[Strength]].
* [[Prosthesis-Wearer Heirloom]], a Heirloom talisman that raises [[Dexterity]].
* [[Stargazer Heirloom]], a Heirloom talisman that raises [[Intelligence]].
* [[Two Fingers Heirloom]], a Heirloom talisman that raises [[Faith]].

{{Navbox_Talismans}}
[[Category:Talismans]]

talismans.title: Prosthesis-Wearer Heirloom
talismans.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Item | title = Prosthesis-Wearer Heirloom | image = talisman_prosthesis-wearer_heirloom.png | type = Talisman | item_effect = Raises dexterity by 5 | weight = 0.8 | sell_price = 1,000 | obtained = [[Millicent]] }} '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a [[Talismans|talisman]] in {{ER}}. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Description.png|50px|Description]] Description == {{Description |EN= ''A talisman engraved with a scene from a heroic tale.
Raises [[dexterity]].'' ''Though born into the accursed [[Scarlet Rot|rot]], when the [[Malenia|young girl]] encountered her [[Blind Swordsman|mentor]] and his [[Flowing Curved Sword|flowing blade]], she gained wings of unparalleled strength.'' }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|50px|Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Quest reward:''' [[Caelid]] * Obtained from [[Millicent]] in central Caelid, after giving her the [[Unalloyed Gold Needle]] and waiting for her to recover. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effect== Raises [[Dexterity]] by 5. ==See Also== * [[Heirlooms]], for a list of the other Heirloom Talismans. * [[Millicent's Prosthesis]], the other Millicent-granted, Dexterity-boosting Talisman. * [[Dexterity-knot Crystal Tear]], a [[Crystal Tear]] granting +10 when used in the [[Flask of Wondrous Physick]]. {{Navbox Talismans}} [[ru:Наследие Протезоносителя]] [[Category:Talismans]]

talismans.title: Stargazer Heirloom
talismans.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Item | title = Stargazer Heirloom | image = talisman_stargazer_heirloom.png | type = Talisman | item_effect = Raises intelligence by 5 | weight = 0.6 | sell_price = 1,000 | obtained = [[Divine Tower of Liurnia]] }} '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a [[Talismans|talisman]] in {{ER}}. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Description.png|50px|Description]] Description == {{Description |EN= ''A talisman engraved with the legend of a queen.
Raises intelligence.'' ''The young astrologer gazed at the night sky as she walked. She had always chased the stars every step of her journey. Then she met the full moon — and, in time, the astrologer became [[Queen Rennala|a queen]].'' }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|50px|Acquisition]] Acquisition== * Obtained from a corpse lying at the top of the [[Divine Tower of Liurnia]], in eastern [[Liurnia of the Lakes]]. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effect== Raises [[Intelligence]] by 5. ==See Also== * [[Heirlooms]] {{Navbox Talismans}} [[ru:Наследие звездочётов]] [[Category:Talismans]]

talismans:missed.title: Talismans
talismans:missed.wikitext: [[File:ER Inventory Menu Icon Talismans.png|thumb]] '''{{PAGENAME}}''' are special items in {{ER}}. They can be equipped to provide a variety of effects. Note that talismans with variants (like +1 or +2 after the name) will only allow one of these to be equipped at a time, so one can't equip both Arsenal Charm and Arsenal Charm +1, even if one has enough talisman slots for both. [[Tarnished (Protagonist)|Tarnished]] begins the game with one talisman slot. This number can be increased up to four by acquiring [[Talisman Pouch|Talisman Pouches]]. {{Clear}} ==All Talismans== {{#lst:All Items (Gallery)|Talismans}}

talismans:missed.title: Aged One's Exultation
talismans:missed.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Item | title = Aged One's Exultation | japanese = 翁の歓喜
(Okina no Kanki) | image = ER Icon Talisman Aged One's Exultation.png | type = Talisman | item_effect = Madness in the vicinity increases attack power | weight = 0.9 | sell_price = 500 }} The '''Aged One's Exultation''' is a [[Talismans (Shadow of the Erdtree)|talisman]] in {{SotE}}. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Description.png|50px|Description]] Description == {{Description |EN = A talisman depicting the exultation of the [[Aging Untouchable|aging untouchable]], whose head resembles a sprig of ripe [[Shabriri Grape|grapes]]. Raises attack power when madness is triggered in the vicinity. "Gift your madness to our [[Lord of Frenzied Flame|Lord]]. Bless our brethren with grapes. Take care that they fully ripen." |JP= 完熟したブドウにも似た 触れ得ざる翁の喜びを象ったタリスマン 周囲で発狂状態が発生すると、攻撃力が高まる 我らが王に、発狂を捧げよ 皆にブドウを与えたまえ 皆のブドウを、熟させたまえ }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|50px|Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Guaranteed Drop:''' [[Abyssal Woods]] * Aged One's Exultation is dropped by the Aging Untouchable found nearest to the [[Abandoned Church]] in the [[Abyssal Woods]]. The Aging Untouchable is only vulnerable after it has been parried.[[Torn Diary Page]] ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effect== Increases [[Attack Power]] by '''+20%''' ('''+12%''' in PvP) for 30 seconds when someone in vicinity to the wearer (7 meters) is afflicted with [[Madness]], including the wearer themself. ==[[File:ER Main Menu Icon Messages.png|50px|Notes]] Notes== * The Aged's One Exulation stacks with the [[Black Dumpling]] and the [[Madding Hand (weapon)|Madding Hand]] {{SOTE}} when Madness is triggered for a total Attack Power increase of '''+41.9%''' for 30 seconds. * Can be [[Leave Item|left]] for other players. * Can be [[Discard Item|discarded]]. * Can be [[Sell Item|sold]] to merchants for {{R}} 500. ==Trivia== * The talisman depicts an [[Aging Untouchable]]. ==See also== * [[Cla

Bloodsucking Cracked Tear
crystalTears:missed.wikitext:
{{Infobox Item |image= ER Icon Crystal Tear Bloodsucking Cracked Tear.png |title= | japanese = 吸血の割れ雫
(Kyūketsu no Wareshizuku) |duration = 180s |item_effect=Enhances attacks at the cost of HP in mixed physick |type=Crystal Tear |max_stored= |obtained= }} '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a [[Tears (SoTE)|tear]] in {{ER}}. ==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description== {{Description |EN= A crystal tear formed slowly over the ages, where the scattered sap of the Scadutree pools deep within the furnace golems. Can be mixed in the Flask of Wondrous Physick. The resulting concoction temporarily increases attack power but drains HP over time. |JP= 飛散した影樹の樹液が
焼炉のゴーレムの底に溜まり
永き時の末に生じた、結晶の雫 「霊薬の聖杯瓶」に配合し
一定時間、攻撃力を高めるが
ＨＰが減り続ける }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Guaranteed drop:''' [[Ruins of Unte]] ([[Scadu Altus]]) * Defeat the [[Furnace Golem]] blocking the entrance to [[Ruins of Unte]]. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effect== Increases damage by '''20%''' ('''12.5%''' against other players) but drains [[HP]] equal to ('''0.2%''' of maximum HP + '''20''') every second for '''180''' seconds. ==[[File:ER Main Menu Icon Messages.png|40px|Notes]] Notes== * [[Invincibility Frames]] can "dodge" the HP loss ticks, including dodge rolls. [[Category:Crystal Tears]] [[Category:Tools]]

crystalTears:missed.title: Cerulean Crystal Tear
crystalTears:missed.wikitext:
{{Infobox_Item | title = Cerulean Crystal Tear | japanese = 青色の結晶雫
(Aoiro no Kesshō Shizuku) | image = ER Icon tear Cerulean Crystal (Left).png | type = Key Item | sub_type = Crystal Tear | item_effect = Restores half of total FP in mixed physick }} '''Cerulean Crystal Tear''' is a [[Crystal Tear]] in {{ER}}. Two instances of this tear exist. ==[[File: ER Custom Icon Description.png|40px|link=Item Description]] Description== {{Description |EN= A crystal tear formed slowly over the ages where the Erdtree's bounty falls to the ground. Can be mixed in the Flask of Wondrous Physick. The resulting concoction restores half of one's maximum FP. |JP= 黄金樹の恵みが降り注ぐ地に
永き時の末に生じた、結晶の雫 「霊薬の聖杯瓶」に配合し
最大ＦＰの半分を回復する }} ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Acquisition.png|40px|link=Acquisition]] Acquisition== '''Guaranteed drop:''' [[Minor Erdtree (West Liurnia)|Minor Erdtree]] ([[Liurnia of the Lakes]]) / [[Minor Erdtree (Mountaintops of the Giants)|Minor Erdtree]] ([[Mountaintops of the Giants]]) * Left version: dropped by the Erdtree Avatar at the Minor Erdtree in southwestern Liurnia of the Lakes, along with one of the [[Ruptured Crystal Tear]]s. * Right version: dropped by the Erdtree Avatar at the Minor Erdtree in eastern Mountaintops of the Giants, along with the [[Crimson Bubbletear]]. ==[[File:ER Custom Icon Effect.png|50px|Effect]] Effect== When mixed in the [[Flask of Wondrous Physick]], it restores [[FP]] equal to '''50%''' of maximum FP on use. If both instances of the tear are used, '''100%''' of FP is restored. ===Gallery=== ER Icon tear Cerulean Crystal (Left).png|Liurnia of the Lakes Cerulean Crystal Tear ER Icon tear Cerulean Crystal (Right).png|Mountaintops of the Giants Cerulean Crystal Tear [[ru:Лазурная кристальная слеза]]

'''Greathood''' is an [[armor|armor piece]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==

{{Description
|EN=Hood far larger than the head it is meant to cover. A burial
shroud of sorts for those who discover, at long last, the truth they
sought.

Increases intelligence and faith to the detriment of HP.

"Yes, surely this is the moon that young Rennala gazed upon."

|JP=頭よりも遥かに大きいフード
追い求めた真実に出会ったとき
それに包まれ、死んでいくための装束

ＨＰと引き換えに、知力と信仰を高める

ああ、幼きレナラは
確かにこの月を見上げたのだ
}}
== Acquisition ==
Having seen [["Sorcerer" Painting]] is required in order to get that item. Found on the ground after reaching the place that is depicted in the painting, on a bridge west of [[Mountaintops of the Giants]].

== Notes ==
* Increases both [[Intelligence]] and [[Faith]] by 2, at the cost of a 10% [[HP]] penalty. 
** The large HP penalty generally makes this head piece not really worth it at more than 40 [[Vigor]], complicated further by how late it can be acquired. Instead, a more focused head piece with a penalty to [[Stamina]] or [[FP]] is more worth it since those can generally be countered with less of a stat investment. 
*** At 60 Vigor it will lower maximum health from 1900 to 1710, roughly the same amount of health as 50 Vigor. At 50 Vigor it will lower maximum health from 1704 to 1534, the same amount of health as 43 Vigor. At 40 Vigor it will lower maximum health from 1450 to 1305, the same amount of health as 37 Vigor. 
*** Good alternatives are the [[Hierodas Glintstone Crown]] for +2 Intelligence (and +2 [[Endurance]]), as its FP penalty will always be outweighed if at or below the [[Mind]] soft

Nothing more." |JP_Name=大司祭の帽子 |JP_Source=[[1.12]] |JP=大司祭、ユミル卿の帽子 頭上の真円は、大いなる意志
その暗黒の深淵を現しており
知力と神秘を高める レラーナの魔術教授であった男は
だが、月への忠誠を棄てていた それはただ、我らの近くにあったにすぎぬ |CN_Name= |CN_Source= |CN= |DE_Name= |DE_Source= |DE= |FR_Name= |FR_Source= |FR= |IT_Name= |IT_Source= |IT= |KR_Name= |KR_Source= |KR= |PL_Name= |PL_Source= |PL= |BR_Name= |BR_Source= |BR= |RU_Name= |RU_Source= |RU= |ES_Name= |ES_Source= |ES= |TH_Name= |TH_Source= |TH= |TW_Name= |TW_Source= |TW= }} ==Acquisition== '''Guaranteed Drop:''' [[Cathedral of Manus Metyr]] ([[Scadu Altus]]) *Acquired after defeating [[Count Ymir, Mother of Fingers]]. **To access the bossfight, the player must first defeat [[Metyr, Mother of Fingers]], then interact with Ymir's throne in the cathedral. **This will make [[Jolan, Swordhand of Night]] appear, who will be hostile and proceed to attack the player. **Vanquishing Jolan will then get Ymir to appear by the throne to

'''Alberich's Pointed Hat''' is a [[helm]] [[armor]] in {{ER}}

== Description ==
{{Description
|EN_line1= [[Mad Tongue Alberich]]'s pointed hat, a sign of a heretical practitioner.
|EN_line2= Set with [[Glintstone#Red Glintstone|red glintstones]], said to be formed by the [[Blood Loss|blood]] of sacrifices. Strengthens [[Thorn Sorceries|thorn sorcery]].
|EN_line3= Alberich was an aloof yet disturbed heretical [[sorcerer]] said to have been driven mad by jeering tongues during his service to the [[Roundtable Hold]] long ago.
|JP_line1= 狂い舌、アルベリッヒの帽子異端の魔術師を示す尖り帽
|JP_line2= 生贄の血に生じるという赤い輝石が埋め込まれており茨の魔術を強化する
|JP_line3= 孤高にして異端の、病んだ魔術師はかつて円卓のために、舌に狂ったという
}}

== Acquisition ==
'''Loot''': [[Leyndell, Royal Capital]]
* Alberich's Pointed Hat is found in the entry hall of the [[Fortified Manor]] in Leyndell, located on the [[Altus Plateau]].

==Notes==
* This item may be [[Armor Adjustment|modified]] into [[Alberich's Pointed Hat (Altered)]].

==See also==
* [[Alberich's Bracers]]
* [[Alberich's Robe]]
* [[Alberich's Robe (Altered)]]
* [[Sage Hood]]
* [[Snow Witch Hat]]

'''Alberich's Pointed Hat (Altered)''' is an [[Armor|armor piece]] found in ''[[Elden Ring (game)|Elden Ring]]''. It is the altered form of [[Alberich's Pointed Hat]].

== Description ==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Mad Tongue Alberich's pointed hat, a sign of a heretical practitioner.

|EN_line2= Alberich was an aloof yet disturbed heretical sorcerer said to have been driven mad by jeering tongues during his service to the Roundtable Hold long ago.

|JP_line1= 狂い舌、アルベリッヒの帽子異端の魔術師を示す尖り帽

|JP_line2= 孤高にして異端の、病んだ魔術師はかつて円卓のために、舌に狂ったという

}}

== Acquisition ==
The non-altered version can be found alongside the rest of the armor set in the Fortified Manor's entry hall, [[Leyndell, Royal Capital]].

{{Infobox Armor

'''Albinauric Mask''' is an [[armor|armor piece]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
{{Description

|EN_line1= Mask made from the largely unaltered hide of a young Albinauric.

|EN_line2= Raises arcane, but reduces the HP recovery effects of the Flask of Crimson Tears.

|EN_line3= A far cry from godskin, this Albinauric hide mask is the product of malicious mockery.

|JP_line1= 若きしろがね人の生皮をそのまま覆面としたもの

|JP_line2= 神秘が高まるが緋雫の聖杯瓶の回復量が少なくなる

|JP_line3= 神肌ならぬ、しろがね肌の覆面は嘲弄的悪意の産物であろう

}}

== Acquisition ==
Found on a corpse near an [[Omenkiller]] in the same building as the Guest Hall grace in [[Volcano Manor]]. It is accessed by jumping on a balcony with a visible opened door.

[[ru:Маска альбинора]]

'''Commoner's Garb''' is an [[armor|armor piece]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
{{Description
|EN=
''Garb with a thick embroidered cape attached. Standard wear for commoners of the Lands Between.''

''The board hung from the neck depicts a sprawling tree, its roots and branches forming two holes. This is a self-imposed shackle, a voluntary display of allegiance to the Erdtree that increases faith.''
|JP=厚手の刺繍マントを纏った服 狭間の地の、あり触れた市民の装束

首から掛けた、穴あきの樹板は 自らが黄金樹の民であること示す 自縛の枷であるといい、信仰を高める
}}

== Acquisition ==
Chance to drop for Commoner enemies.

==Notes==
* The altered version description slightly differs in the very first sentence.
* Grants +1 to [[Faith]] when worn. 

==See Also==

* [[Commoner's Garb (Altered)]]
* [[Commoner's Simple Garb]]

'''Alberich's Robe''' is an [[armor|armor piece]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
{{Description
|EN=

''Mad Tongue Alberich's robe.''

''Set with red glintstones, said to be formed by the blood of sacrifices. Strengthen thorn sorcery.''

''Alberich was an aloof yet disturbed heretical sorcerer said to have been driven mad by jeering tongues during his service to the Roundtable Hold long ago.''
|JP=
狂い舌、アルベリッヒの装束

生贄の血に生じるという 赤い輝石が埋め込まれており 茨の魔術を強化する

孤高にして異端の、病んだ魔術師は かつて円卓のために、舌に狂ったという
}}

'''All-Knowing Armor''' is an [[armor|armor piece]] found in {{ER}}.

== Description ==
{{Description
|EN=
Armor set with countless eyes and ears. Worn by Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing.

Knowledge begins with the recognition of one's ignorance. The realization that the search for knowledge is unending. But when Gideon glimpsed into the will of Queen Marika, he shuddered in fear.

At the end that should not be.
|JP=
無数の目と耳が散りばめられた胴鎧 百智卿、ギデオン＝オーフニールの装束

知とは、自らの無知を知ることであり 知ることの終わりなきを知ることである だが、女王マリカの遺志に触れた時 ギデオンは恐れてしまった

あるはずのない終わりを
}}

== Acquisition ==
*Defeat [[Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing]].

==Notes==
* The altered version has the exact same description as the normal version.
